item.title: The Monaco GP only just finished, sort of. Plus: Lando Norris leads early in Barcelona
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: McLaren, Red Bull lodging intentions to appeal FIA ruling on Pierre Gasly
item.description: Isack Hadjar has lost his first podium finish with Red Bull and McLaren's Oscar Piastri moves from fourth to fifth after the decision.

item.title: Pierre Gasly regains Monaco GP podium after time penalties overturned: How did it unfold?
item.description: The Alpine driver crossed the finish line third but was demoted to seventh, and will now regain his first podium finish since Brazil 2024.

item.title: An accident denied Robert Kubica his Ferrari dream, so he made up for it at Le Mans
item.description: A crash derailed Robert Kubica's early F1 career but the journey back to racing led him to success at the famed 24 Hours of Le Mans.

item.title: F1 drivers welcome engine rule changes from 2027 but warn ‘it’s not going to change the world’
item.description: World champion Lando Norris and Max Verstappen agreed with Sergio Perez that the new F1 engine agreement was a step in the right direction.

item.title: F1 mailbag: How did Mercedes mess up with Russell? Are Red Bull happy with Hadjar?
item.description: From George Russell's zero points to Isack Hadjar's surprise third, our experts answer your questions after the Monaco Grand Prix.

item.title: F1 agrees to change controversial engine rules for 2027 and 2028
item.description: Red Bull's Max Verstappen described this season's rules as "anti-racing."

item.title: Best campaign in recent F1 memory? Plus: George Russell's pace problem
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Nineteen U.S. attorneys general voice concerns to F1, FIA over tobacco industry sponsorship
item.description: McLaren and Ferrari have tobacco-related sponsors, nicotine pouch brands Zyn and Velo.

item.title: Two more athletes sue Puma, Mercedes F1 over claim shoes resulted in 'severe and permanent' injuries
item.description: Damion Thomas Jr. and Champion Allison are the latest athletes to sue the two companies in the U.S.

item.title: Lewis Hamilton addresses Kim Kardashian relationship for first time, thanks her for ‘support’ at Monaco
item.description: Kardashian attended the Monaco Grand Prix on Sunday to support the Ferrari driver.

item.title: Ferrari did almost everything right in Monaco. Why wasn't it enough to win?
item.description: Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc's days wildly diverged, but they still share the same problem: the car isn't up to a fight with Mercedes

item.title: What is ADUO? The F1 rule may give struggling teams a second chance
item.description: F1's catch-up mechanism could help level the playing field – or exacerbate it.

item.title: Red Bull has F1's best engine, FIA finds, allowing rivals to unlock upgrades
item.description: The ruling freezes Red Bull's engine while rivals get development upgrades — up to four over the next two seasons

item.title: Kimi Antonelli made Monaco look easy, and his rivals are running out of answers
item.description: Kimi Antonelli's Monaco win puts him in 2026 F1 title control. But his rival George Russell has more to worry about than just the points gap

item.title: F1 drivers criticize track breakdown at Monaco: 'That kind of stuff shouldn't be happening'
item.description: The red flag was waved on Lap 68 as damage to the track surface at the final corner was inspected.

item.title: Sergio Pérez Monaco penalty strips Cadillac of first F1 point
item.description: Sergio Pérez has been handed a post-race penalty that has cost the Cadillac team its first Formula 1 point.

item.title: Alpine launches bid to overturn Pierre Gasly’s Monaco F1 penalties
item.description: Gasly crossed the line third but was demoted to seventh place after receiving two five-second time penalties for speeding in the pit lane.

item.title: Monaco GP: Submit your questions for our F1 mailbag
item.description: What’s on your mind after the race? Send us your questions and we’ll get to them in this week's mailbag.

item.title: Ferrari's Charles Leclerc says he 'looks like an idiot' after crashing out of Monaco Grand Prix
item.description: Leclerc crashed out on lap 68 at the final corner at his home grand prix.

item.title: Monaco Grand Prix briefing: Track breaks up, top drivers bow out, Kimi Antonelli wins fifth straight
item.description: A late red flag stoppage made things dicey, but F1's 19-year-old points leader held off Lewis Hamilton for another win

item.title: George Russell left 'bamboozled' by his drop in pace after good start to F1 season
item.description: The Mercedes driver has not stood on the podium since finishing second in Shanghai in March and finished in sixth on Saturday's qualifying.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli takes statement pole for the Monaco GP
item.description: Kimi Antonelli secured pole position for the 2026 Monaco Grand Prix, beating out Max Verstappen by 0.043-seconds. It will be an...

item.title: F1 drivers always say Monaco is special. But only a hot lap of the track proves it
item.description: The Monaco Grand Prix is a unique driving experience, which in recent years F1 has opened up to luck passengers too

item.title: Ferrari's Fred Vasseur 'under observation' in hospital, misses F1 Monaco Grand Prix qualifying
item.description: The Italian team released a statement on Saturday morning announcing the development.

item.title: Welcome to F1's new era of 'speed chess,' where every pass comes with a price
item.description: DRS is out. Overtake Mode is in. Here's why drivers say passing just got a lot harder

item.title: Why F1's boiling drama over engine compression ratios matters for the 2026 season
item.description: Has Mercedes found a loophole with its engines? And what exactly is the loophole, anyway?

item.title: F1's new cars behave unlike any others. It's all down to new aerodynamics
item.description: F1 has new cars in 2026, which look different to previous eras in grand prix racing. Here's how they work and what the drivers think

item.title: F1 hits reset in 2026. Your guide to the new cars, new rules and new era
item.description: Formula 1 evolves dramatically in 2026. Here's your year-long guide to it all

item.title: F1's new engines are the sport's biggest gamble in 12 years. Here's what changed
item.description: F1 is introducing new hybrid engines ahead of the 2026 season. Here's how they work and why they matter

item.title: How Formula 1's cost cap works: Rules, penalties and past breaches explained
item.description: The sport's $135 million spending limit aims to create parity — but compliance isn't always simple

item.title: F1 penalty points: How they work and why they're needed even though drivers have doubts
item.description: A deep dive into the details - and arguments - of Formula One penalty points for driver infractions

item.title: What are F1 driver development programs? Inside the academies that nurture future champions
item.description: All of F1's new 2025 drivers have one thing in common - they were part of a team’s driver development program or academy.

item.title: What are F1’s racing rules? A look at race stewards and drivers' battle for clarity
item.description: Multiple times during the 2024 season, drivers have faced questions — and raised their own — about what is and is not allowed on track.

item.title: How F1's Red Bull mastered the art of the 2-second pit stop
item.description: A deep dive into the positions, the practice, and the fine-tuned production that is the Formula One pit stop.

item.title: Welcome to Between the Racing Lines, your new guide to all things F1
item.description: F1 is complicated and confusing at times, and is constantly evolving. Here's your guide to keeping up.

item.title: Sim time, data crunching and Candy Crush: The life of an F1 reserve driver
item.description: Reserve drivers are more than simple substitutes — they play important roles within their teams, even if they rarely get to hit the track.

item.title: How F1 drivers train physically and mentally to go from 'gym fit' to 'race fit'
item.description: To have a chance at the title, drivers need not just a top-caliber car but to extract the maximum performance from it — and themselves.
